mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 260 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days as investors price in delayed tightening.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the series fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising FEDFUNDS and declining CPIAUCSL signals effective monetary policy tightening that reduces equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp and CPIAUCSL falls > 0.3pp in 1 month
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in 50%+ of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days as corporate earnings expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines by 2%+ over 2 months, or the series shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in PAYEMS above 150k signals strong labor market dynamics, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days through consumer spending optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the series fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in HOUST above 1350 signals housing market strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days through correlated consumer and corporate investment activity.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1350, or the series shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDPC1 and INDPRO signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days through elevated corporate earnings and risk appetite.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises > 1% YoY and INDPRO rises > 1% MoM
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in 50%+ of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in UNRATE below 3.5% signals labor market tightness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days as inflationary pressures force rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%, or the series fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.03).
